mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show a Rank IC below 0.03 with month-over-month PAYEMS changes over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative in more than 50% of triggered months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the mean return during these months is not below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production and acceleration in consumer prices captures a stagflationary supply shock that compresses profit margins and valuation multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term Rank IC exceeds -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate raise discount rates faster than earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months during tightening cycles, or the Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and 5-day returns is above zero in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that reduces revenue expectations for consumer-facing sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not negative following month-over-month RSAFS declines in more than 50% of instances, or the mean post-decline return is not below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production confirm a manufacturing contraction that precedes broad-based earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines in more than 50% of episodes, or the mean 60-day return during these episodes is above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and the unemployment rate reveal a restrictive monetary policy mistake that raises financing costs while weakening household demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS and rising UNRATE fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint-signal months, or the interaction Rank IC exceeds -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns show a Rank IC below 0.03 with quarterly GDPC1 growth over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns do not follow GDPC1 growth in more than 50% of quarters.
